---
title: Interactive demo
sidebar: main
page-navigation: true
---

Run a demo version of pySLAMMER in your browser — no install required. The demo below uses [marimo](https://marimo.io)'s WASM build to load pySLAMMER straight into a Pyodide kernel; pick a sample ground motion, scale it, choose an analysis method, and adjust the slope parameters to see how each affects sliding displacement.

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## Beyond the demo

pySLAMMER's real value emerges when used programmatically with Python. See the [analysis-methods comparison](rigid_flex.qmd) and [batch simulations](batch_simulations.qmd) examples for workflows that go beyond what widgets can show.
:::

::: {.column-page-right}
```{=html}
<iframe
  src="https://molab.marimo.io/github/pySLAMMER/pySLAMMER/blob/main/notebooks/pyslammer_demo.py/wasm?show-code=false"
  width="100%" height="900"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px;"
  loading="lazy"
  title="pySLAMMER demo (interactive)"></iframe>
```
:::


## Explore further

If you want to explore the demo further, you can **open in molab**, create a fork and edit the notebook. Or you can **download the notebook** it and run it locally with `marimo edit pyslammer_demo.py`.

[Open in molab (interactive)](https://molab.marimo.io/github/pySLAMMER/pySLAMMER/blob/main/notebooks/pyslammer_demo.py/wasm?show-code=false){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
[Download notebook (.py)](https://github.com/pySLAMMER/pySLAMMER/blob/main/notebooks/pyslammer_demo.py){.btn .btn-outline-secondary role="button"}
